author: @Gwydion67
text:
You already invited me to visit you once I'm around England, Rob.
If I knew of your multi-Country journey, including Germany earlier, I surely would have invited you to my place, also. You would have loved our "Franconian Swiss" and the town of Nuremberg (Nürnberg).
Anyway, Köln is quite a nice town, both beautiful, lovely, full of life and full of inspiration (like your thoughts on tidal power usage from the Rhine prove).

Thanks for sharing your impressions! 😃👍

author: @shtfengineering7472
text: Knowing that you complied with the tyranny in order to travel makes me sad.  I misjudged your character.  It is a choice.  Hard for some, easy for others.  I may never travel again and I have decided to be OK with that.  Because it is being pushed upon us, I will never.  I have the utmost respect for you as a scientist and researcher.

author: @groovedodger

author: @kimkeam2094
text: Great to see you able to get around. We are currently in lockdown in Victoria Australia which means we cannot leave our area more than 5 kilometres. We don’t have COVID and have had both shots of Pfizer and can only leave our house once a week for shopping. As far as we are aware there are no people infected within our area at all. If you leave Australia now it is unlikely that you can return. Most flights are cancelled so seats are hard to get and on return you have to isolate in a designated hotel for 14 days at your cost. If you have been cleared then you can go home.

replies.author: @William_Hada
replies.text: There is no graphene oxide of any sort in any of the vaccines. The magnet challenge is a baseless internet Meme staged to get TikTok views. Spreading misinformation like that is dangerous because it makes people more afraid of getting vaccinated.

author: @azlandpilotcar4450
text:
I have a vertical "panoramic" set of photos of the  of the Dom taken (by me on 35mm film) in 1977with scaffolding under restoration.  I have another from the middle 1980s taken with the same camera.  My mom took a similar set in the 1990s.  
The limestone acid rain damage and soot (attributed to coal and fuel oil in the 1970s) was fixed in the 1980s, but they were still working on it.  Glad to see that Koln is maintaining their long running hobby of working on the Dom.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: lol - it is a never ending task is my guess lol
